candidates.name: Gretchen Whitmer
candidates.notes: Whitmer scores 2/70. Most nationally prominent figure in this set alongside Pritzker. Championed Prop 3 abortion amendment. Survived 2020 kidnapping plot by militia members. Eucharist-mocking video controversy damaged Catholic relations (Oct 2024). NRA F throughout career. Term-limited — leaving Jan 2027. Potential 2028 presidential candidate. COVID lockdown controversies made her polarizing nationally.

profile.religion: Christian (denomination unspecified; raised in faith but does not foreground it; Eucharist-mocking controversy with Catholic community Oct 2024)
profile.education: B.A. Communications, Michigan State University (1993); J.D., Michigan State University College of Law (1998)
profile.background: Attorney; served in Michigan House (2001-2006) and Michigan Senate (2006-2015); became 49th governor in 2019.

candidates.name: Gary Peters
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 0%/6% lifetime. NRA F. Episcopalian. Navy Reserve veteran. Announced retirement Jan 2025 — not seeking reelection. His open seat creates a major 2026 Michigan Senate battleground. Rated most effective senator 2021-22. Quiet workhorse rather than media figure. Score 2/70.

profile.education: B.A. Political Science, Alma College (1980); M.B.A., University of Detroit Mercy (1984); J.D., Wayne State University Law School (1989); M.A. Philosophy, Michigan State University (2007)
profile.background: Former U.S. House member and Michigan State Senator. Navy Reserve officer. Former investment advisor and lottery commissioner. Former DSCC chairman.
profile.office: 724 Hart Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 477 Michigan Ave., Suite 1837, Patrick V. McNamara Federal Building
110 Michigan St. NW, Suite 720, Gerald R. Ford Federal Building

aipac-track.title: TrackAIPAC — Michigan delegation
aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

aipac-endorsements.title: AIPAC Election Center — endorsed candidates
aipac-endorsements.excerpt: AIPAC's own public endorsement list. If a candidate appears here, they have accepted AIPAC backing; the RESOLUTE Citizen scoring criterion treats this as a failure on America-First question 3.

candidates.name: Elissa Slotkin
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 0%, lifetime 2%. NRA F. Jewish. CIA analyst with three Iraq tours — strongest intelligence community credentials in Senate. Unusual position: favors blocking offensive weapon sales to Israel despite being Jewish. Grew up in gun-owning family, trained on Glock/M4 in Iraq, but supports full gun control suite. Won competitive 2024 race narrowly. Score 2/70. Her CIA/Pentagon background makes her unique among Democrats — a national security Democrat in the Scoop Jackson tradition.

profile.education: B.A. Sociology, Cornell University (2002); M.A. International Relations, Columbia University (2003)
profile.background: Former CIA analyst with three Iraq tours and acting Assistant Secretary of Defense. Worked on White House NSC for Presidents Bush and Obama. Former U.S. House member.
profile.office: 291 Russell Senate Office Building
